items.rationale: Sees stablecoins as crypto's clearest product-market fit and the first credible path to onboard a billion people; activity uncorrelated to trading volume signals organic use, cheap/instant settlement, and powerful merchant-consumer network effects.

citations.quote: Stablecoins now present what I believe is the first credible opportunity to onboard a billion people into crypto.
What makes me most excited about stablecoins is the fact that all this activity seems to be uncorrelated with broader crypto trading volume
Stablecoins have found product market fit and this chart shows it.

items.rationale: Believes AI agents need access to an economy and that crypto rails are a natural fit; if AI agents become primary users, many wallet UX problems disappear. Backs infra builders like Catena Labs.
items.age_note: Theme persistent since Dec 2024; restated and acted on May–June 2025, so treated as current.

citations.quote: Crypto rails are a natural fit for AI agents, which will need access to an economy to unlock their full potential.
if AI agents become the primary users of crypto, a lot of the UX challenges...go away.

items.rationale: His signature thesis: only 5-10% of ~617M crypto owners actually transact (30-60M users), so the biggest growth lever is converting dormant holders rather than expanding total investors.
items.age_note: Stated since early 2024; reinforced through 2025 metrics, current.

citations.quote: Let's close the gap between crypto owners and active on-chain users. This is more important...than expanding the total number of crypto investors.
I believe this is the year to re-engage the 617M "passive" crypto holders and bring the world onchain.

items.rationale: His favorite adoption metric; repeated all-time highs (35M users), and improvements like account abstraction (EIP-7702) and embedded wallets (Privy, Turnkey, Dynamic) make now the best time to build a next-gen wallet.

citations.quote: Wallet infrastructure has significantly improved...Now is a better time than ever to build a next-generation mobile wallet.
Mobile wallet users is one of my favorite metrics to track in the crypto space.

items.rationale: Views years of infra work bringing fees below a penny on Solana and Base/Ethereum L2s as the unlock for stablecoins and the next wave of consumer apps; infra improvements unlock new applications.

citations.quote: This is a great example of how infrastructure improvements unlock new applications, and I'm excited to see what else might get unlocked.
USDC transfers on Ethereum L2s now cost less than a penny...Now is the time to build the next big consumer app in crypto.

items.rationale: Sees AI/vibe-coding democratizing game creation while payments/distribution remain extractive; games are the product most suited to stablecoin microtransactions, and IP becomes a key differentiator. Backed Oncade, Launcher/Punkland.

citations.quote: Games are perhaps the product most obviously suited to stablecoin-based payment rails – driving billions of microtransactions annually
New AI tools and vibe coding platforms could have a profound impact on the gaming industry

items.rationale: Only 22 tokens have >$1M monthly net revenue; expects new regulatory environment and market-structure legislation to let projects 'complete the economic loop' and accrue real revenue to tokens.

citations.quote: the path is finally opening up for tokens to complete the economic loop...leading to a much healthier token economy.

items.rationale: DEX-to-CEX spot volume rising (to ~17%, +51% YoY), which he reads as the maturation of the DeFi ecosystem as more people come onchain; notes Coinbase adding native DEX trading.

citations.quote: The increasing ratio highlights the overall development of the DeFi ecosystem.

items.rationale: Calls it underrated and key to bringing a billion people onchain by importing contributors with incentives exogenous to the protocol (e.g., ESPN resolving prediction markets, proof-of-personhood).
items.age_note: Last emphasized Aug 2024; not restated in 2025, so persisted.

citations.quote: I'm now convinced that web proofs/zkTLS will play a critical role in getting us there. It's all about incentives.
zkTLS / zkEmail is an underrated category in crypto.

items.rationale: Powerful tool for surfacing real-time information; argued US participation makes outputs more accurate. Strong 2024 election-cycle interest.
items.age_note: Heavily discussed mid-late 2024 around the US election; no 2025 restatement.

citations.quote: Prediction markets have proven to be a powerful tool for surfacing real time information.

items.rationale: Reads rising BTC/ETH ETP net flows (+28%) and a likely pending spot Solana ETF as signs of industry maturity as regulation clears and distributors activate.

citations.quote: Institutional capital entering crypto is a sign of the industry's overall maturity.

items.rationale: AI will >1,000x online content; we lack infrastructure (curation, provenance, IP) to handle it, and blockchains can provide the open global coordination layer. Excited by Story Protocol.
items.age_note: Peaked mid-2024 (Story); echoed June 2025 in gaming-IP context but lighter.

citations.quote: We need an open, global coordination layer in order for AI generated content to thrive. And this is exactly what blockchains can provide.

items.rationale: The very large gap between 617M owners and only 30-60M monthly transacting users is both his opportunity and his worry — most owners remain dormant and active-address counts are easily gamed.

citations.quote: Only 5-10% of crypto owners are transacting users.
This differential suggests there is a huge opportunity to convert existing crypto owners — who are mostly passive holders — into active users.

items.rationale: Total transaction fees fell 43% YoY in 2025; while partly intentional fee reduction, he flags it as a nuanced negative for aggregate real economic demand and notes active debate around it/REV.

citations.quote: Total transaction fees (demand for blockspace): -43%
we've seen lots of debates on X around the importance of this metric (and related metrics, like REV).

items.rationale: Active addresses have become a bad proxy for users due to low fees and incentives to game; reliable user measurement requires heavy filtering, complicating adoption claims.

citations.quote: Active addresses have lately become a really bad measure for users (low fees + incentives to game).

items.rationale: Worries that embedding wallets too deeply into the app stack risks abstracting away the self-custody/web3 vision, misaligning with crypto's core value.
items.age_note: Raised as an open question July 2024; never restated.

citations.quote: There is a real risk of over-abstracting wallets and blockchains from users.

items.rationale: Sees doubling wholesale CBDC development post-sanctions as a threat to dollar dominance and argues USD stablecoins should be embraced; keeping crypto onshore is framed as national security.
items.age_note: Argued Mar & Dec 2024; not restated in 2025.

citations.quote: This is a national security concern, since the dollar's status as a reserve currency gives the United States tremendous power.
Keeping crypto onshore is a matter of national security.

items.rationale: Concerned that a few large companies control compute, data, and curation algorithms; consolidation is 'worrisome' and crypto may be the only credible counterbalance.

citations.quote: This consolidation is worrisome. Crypto might be the only credible counterbalancing force out there.

items.rationale: Frames meme coins dismissively as the next evolution of the gambling industry rather than core value — consistent with his view that speculation is a byproduct, not the point.

citations.quote: Meme coins are just the next evolution of the gambling industry.
speculation is a byproduct that is separate from its core value proposition.

items.rationale: One of the two leaders in stablecoin issuance; Circle's NYSE IPO and sub-cent/sub-second L2 transfers reinforce his stablecoin-PMF thesis and the onboarding opportunity.

citations.quote: On the issuer side, it's a two horse race between USDC and Tether.
USDC issuer Circle went public on the New York Stock Exchange

items.rationale: The other dominant stablecoin issuer driving record supply and $128B in treasuries held by stablecoins; central to the global payment-network growth he tracks.

citations.quote: On the issuer side, it's a two horse race between USDC and Tether.

items.rationale: Repeatedly the top chain by active addresses/DEX volume, increasingly capturing stablecoin growth, and a likely pending spot ETF — a low-fee L1 enabling his payments/adoption thesis.

citations.quote: we are seeing some notable growth on chains like Solana, Arbitrum, and Base.
The SEC recently requested spot Solana exchange-traded fund (ETF) issuers to update their S-1 filings

items.rationale: Low-fee L2 (sub-penny USDC transfers) capturing DEX and stablecoin activity; central to his 'infra unlocks consumer apps' narrative.

citations.quote: new Ethereum L2s like Base, we've finally been able to make stablecoins a good product for payments.

items.rationale: Dominant stablecoin settlement layer alongside Tron, beneficiary of rising ETP net flows, and the base for the L2 scaling that underpins cheap payments.

citations.quote: On the infrastructure side, Ethereum and Tron continue to dominate.
June 2025: $45B total ETP net flows ($42B BTC, $3.4B ETH)

items.rationale: Store-of-value benchmark drawing the bulk of ETP net flows ($42B), which he reads as institutional maturation of the asset class.

citations.quote: Stablecoin payments and AI agents are some of the clearest use cases for crypto since Bitcoin's store of value.
June 2025: $45B total ETP net flows ($42B BTC, $3.4B ETH)

items.rationale: Named among chains seeing notable recent stablecoin growth, supporting his low-fee-rails payments thesis.

citations.quote: we are seeing some notable growth on chains like Solana, Arbitrum, and Base.

items.rationale: Cited as a dominant stablecoin settlement chain — relevant to his payments-volume thesis, though noted more as an incumbent than a personal favorite.

citations.quote: On the infrastructure side, Ethereum and Tron continue to dominate.

risk_regime.rationale: Consistently constructive and forward-leaning: declares 2025 'another exciting year,' points to stablecoins, mobile wallets, DEX share, ETP flows and token-revenue all trending up, and frames an improving regulatory/market-structure environment as the unlock for the next adoption wave. He explicitly separates speculation from core value, so his risk-on stance is about long-term adoption and infrastructure rather than price beta. Tempers it only with measurement caveats (bots, declining blockspace fees), keeping it strong-but-not-maximal.

notes: Daren is an adoption/metrics-and-infrastructure thinker at a16z crypto (author of the State of Crypto report), so his 'views' read as data-backed sector theses more than trading calls. Most recent (mid-2025) center of gravity is overwhelmingly STABLECOINS as crypto's killer app and the first credible billion-user onboarding path, tightly coupled with AI agents on crypto rails, onchain gaming, and token value-accrual under expected US market-structure legislation. Persistent older themes (zkTLS/web proofs, prediction markets, AI content coordination/IP, decentralized social, DePIN, DeSci, dollar/CBDC national-security) were genuinely held but not restated in 2025 and are marked persisted with age notes. No reversals detected — his stance has compounded consistently rather than flipped; the main evolution is escalating conviction in stablecoins (from 'killer app' framing in 2024 to 'first credible billion-user onboarding' in June 2025). He expresses no concern about specific tokens (tokens_concerned empty); his nearest token-level skepticism is toward meme coins as a sector (speculation/gambling). Conviction scores reflect intensity and recency, not certainty of outcome.
